page: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark CPU encryption and Geekbench image processing tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks also rank in the top 25%, though the Passmark memory score falls in the middle 50% average.

The lack of local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff, requiring reliance on network storage for disk-heavy operations. However, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, all ranking in the top 25%. It is also capable of handling medium and large language model inference, showing strong prompt processing speeds across 135M, 7B, and 70B models. This hardware profile is suited for enterprise application servers, database hosting, software compilation, and containerized microservices.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it exhibits strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 accelerates workloads like image processing and encryption, where it achieves top-tier benchmark results. While the absence of local NVMe storage requires network-attached alternatives, the instance is highly capable for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dedicated ninety-six vCPU instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced hardware acceleration for enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated cryptography and vector math
Strong multi-core and database benchmark performance, ranking in the top 25%
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
